💡 Key Takeaways
  • Best UL win rate: UL10 at 80.0% (10 trades). Note: low sample size.
  • Best DL win rate: DL8 at 60.0% (25 trades). Note: low sample size.
  • Weakest UL: UL3 (37.6% win rate). Weakest DL: ORL (39.9% win rate).
  • UL ladders at ≥50% win rate: UL4, UL7, UL9, UL10. DL ladders at ≥50%: DL5, DL7, DL8.
  • Reversal setups (RS/RL) average 47.8% win rate vs primary setups (BS/SS) at 41.1% — reversals outperform.
  • Best setup type overall: Reversal Short at 47.9% win rate (1140 trades).

💡 Key Takeaways
  • Overall net P&L across all 7,027 trades: $391,933 (43.6% win rate).
  • Highest total P&L ladder (Longs): UL1 — $66,610 from 1185 trades.
  • Highest total P&L ladder (Shorts): DL1 — $67,200 from 1111 trades.

💡 Key Takeaways
  • ORH→UL1 (first long extension): 41.2% win rate — your most traded UL step.
  • ORL→DL1 (first short extension): 39.9% win rate — your most traded DL step.
  • First UL step to cross 50% win rate: UL7→UL8.
  • First DL step to cross 50% win rate: DL7→DL8.
  • UL statistical confidence drops below 20 trades at: UL6→UL7 — treat extreme-end win rates with caution.
  • DL statistical confidence drops below 20 trades at: DL8→DL9.
  • General pattern: win rate tends to increase at extreme UL/DL levels but sample sizes shrink — more data needed to confirm edge.
💡 Key Takeaways — Daily Reach
  • On 84.3% of days, price extended at least to UL1 from ORH. On 91.5% of days, price extended at least to DL1 from ORL.
  • 50% of UL days reached at least UL2. 50% of DL days reached at least DL2.
  • Most common UL reversal zone: UL1 — price topped out here on 34.1% of UL days (174 days).
  • Most common DL reversal zone: DL1 — price bottomed here on 38.2% of DL days (192 days).
  • ⚠️ Data note: these figures use trade entries as a proxy for price reach. A level only appears if a 3-bar setup triggered there — actual raw price may have extended slightly further on some days.
Cumulative Reach — How far does price go? (% of days reaching AT LEAST this level)
Daily Extreme — Most Common Reversal Zone (% of days this was the highest/lowest level before reversing)
💡 Key Takeaways — Time & Day Patterns
  • Best win-rate hour: 09:00 — 54.6% (590 trades).

  • Worst hour by P&L: 16:00 — $44.

💡 Key Takeaways — Risk & Reward
  • Overall Profit Factor: 2.02 — above 1.0 means gross wins exceed gross losses.
  • Avg Win: $254 (43.1 pts) · Avg Loss: $-97 (-15.4 pts) · R:R: 2.61×.
  • Expected value per trade: $56 (10.1 pts) (positive edge).
  • Best R:R ladder: UL9 at 3.83× (4 trades).
  • Highest expectancy ladder: UL10 at $348/trade (58.7 pts). Lowest: UL8 at $-12/trade (-1.2 pts).
  • Best Profit Factor by setup: Reversal Short at 2.52.
  • Avg MFE on winning trades: $261 · Avg capture efficiency: 96.6% of peak move banked.
  • Avg MAE on losers: $-95 — this is how far losers typically went against before stopping out.
